metadata.language_policy: English for strategy and evaluation; Taiwan Traditional Chinese for stage-language examples.
metadata.canonical_topic: AI 軟體醫材的資安實戰：從美國 FDA 524B 規範到 Threat Modeling 與 Patch SLA 的完整落地

engines.name: Design Engine
engines.function: Convert the CYBERSEC topic into a professional 30-minute conference narrative with timing, authority, and audience cognition built in.

engines.name: Slide Engine
engines.function: Produce a compact Apple-style 14-slide structure with one message, strong visual dominance, and no decorative security imagery.

engines.name: Evaluation Engine
engines.function: Score the macro talk with the 100-point rubric before slide penalties are applied.

engines.name: Constraint Engine
engines.function: Gate every slide with pass/fail slide constraints, anti-AI-feel checks, penalties, and public-safety boundaries.

engines.name: Optimization Engine
engines.function: Repair the highest-leverage weak points, rewrite weak slides, and simulate a second-pass evaluation.

engines.name: Report Engine
engines.function: Emit stable Markdown and CSV outputs for rehearsal, peer review, and spreadsheet scoring.

title_candidates: AI 軟體醫材的資安實戰：從 FDA 524B 到 Threat Modeling 與 Patch SLA
你賣的不是模型：AI 醫材資安、證據鏈與 Patch SLA
Cyber Safety Is Patient Safety：AI SaMD 的資安治理落地
從模型到醫療系統：AI SaMD 的資安證據鏈
法規要的是證據，不是口號：AI 醫材資安實作路線圖
AI SaMD Security Operations：Threat Modeling, Testing, Patch SLA
brief.recommended_title: AI 軟體醫材的資安實戰：從美國 FDA 524B 規範到 Threat Modeling 與 Patch SLA 的完整落地
brief.positioning: A conference-grade operating model for AI SaMD teams that must translate FDA 524B, FDA 2025 premarket cybersecurity expectations, TFDA-facing AI/ML SaMD evidence, testing, and Patch SLA into product decisions.
audience_segments: Medical-device founders and product leaders who need a practical security scope model.
Security engineers and consultants who need to convert findings into evidence and governance decisions.
Regulatory, QA, and clinical-risk teams who need traceability from architecture to controls, tests, fixes, and records.
Hospital and healthcare IT leaders who evaluate vendor trust, service continuity, and patient-safety exposure.
Cross-functional managers who need a shared language for ownership, evidence, and repair timing.
audience_assumptions: They know AI/ML and medical-device language at different depths, but not everyone has connected it to postmarket vulnerability operations.
They expect technical seriousness and practical takeaways, not a regulatory reading session.
They may underestimate how quickly a model demo becomes a product system with identity, data, update, logging, and support obligations.
pain_points: AI security is mistakenly narrowed to model robustness.
Regulatory work is treated as submission paperwork instead of traceable engineering evidence.
Testing produces reports but not decisions, retest proof, or Patch SLA governance.
Small teams assume mature security programs are unreachable and delay minimum evidence work.
Healthcare stakeholders need a patient-safety explanation that avoids fear-heavy examples.
desired_takeaways: Product scale determines cybersecurity evidence burden.
FDA 524B and FDA 2025 can be translated into monitor/identify/address, CVD, updates/patches, SBOM, and traceability work.
TFDA-facing AI/ML SaMD preparation needs model evidence, governance language, and stack-level security boundaries.
White-box and black-box testing are valuable when findings become decisions and retest evidence.
Patch SLA is the operating bridge between vulnerability discovery, product trust, and audit readiness.
brief.core_thesis: AI SaMD security is not a model-security checklist. It is a product trust system: scope the product, trace the evidence, test the exposure, govern findings, and prove repairability.
brief.why_now: AI medical products are moving from standalone model demonstrations into deployed systems: viewers, cloud runtimes, databases, update mechanisms, hospital integrations, and support workflows. That shift makes cybersecurity a product-continuity and patient-safety issue.
brief.speaker_authority_angle: The speaker translates regulatory expectations, AI-stack risk, testing practice, and product operations into one implementation model for teams preparing for real CYBERSEC-level scrutiny.
brief.differentiator: The talk does not list regulations or tools. It uses four product scales, one evidence chain, and one Patch SLA decision loop to make cybersecurity operational.
risk_factors: Regulatory content can become dense and slow.
Technical examples can drift toward tool catalogs.
Slides 6 and 7 can overload the audience if they become control lists.
The ending can be weakened if the speaker spends too long on testing details.
Any private implementation detail would reduce trust and public-safety readiness.
risk_mitigations: Compress regulation into evidence-chain logic: Risk -> Control -> Test -> Fix -> Evidence.
Use product-scale comparisons instead of vulnerability lists.
Make Slide 10 an intentional recovery beat after the dense middle.
Use cut markers before speaking faster.
Keep all examples public-safe and artifact-level.

narrative.why_this_mode: The audience likely begins with the false simplification that AI SaMD security is model security or compliance paperwork. This structure breaks that myth, replaces it with product-scale reality, then gives a practical operating model.
narrative.opening_hook: Start with the stage line `你賣的不是模型` after the required title and disclaimer beats. The line immediately shifts the audience from model performance to product trust.
narrative.problem_framing: The security problem grows as AI moves from model artifact to viewer, platform, database, connected medical system, update chain, and support workflow.
narrative.tension: Regulators, hospitals, and users do not only ask whether the model is accurate. They ask whether the product can be monitored, patched, disclosed, tested, explained, and repaired.
development_arc: Slides 4-5 establish urgency and product scale.
Slides 6-7 translate regulation and governance into an evidence chain.
Slides 8-9 apply the model to product-scale risk.
Slide 10 turns the technical argument into patient-safety significance.
Slides 11-12 convert testing into Patch SLA decisions.
Slides 13-14 give an executable path and closing principle.
narrative.climax: Slide 12: every finding needs a decision. This is where testing, regulation, product ownership, and trust converge.
narrative.closing_structure: Slide 13 gives the audience a 30 / 60 / 90 implementation path; Slide 14 closes with trust-before-audit and no new material.
narrative.final_takeaway: 先把安全做進產品，法規文件才會自然長出來。

segments.function: Formal opening and required compliance beat.
Regulatory evidence and local governance bridge.
Testing and Patch SLA operating model.

variants.name: safe
variants.rules: Remove secondary labels from slides 7, 8, 9, and 11; preserve all memory lines.

variants.name: rushed
variants.rules: Slide 6 becomes evidence chain only; slide 7 one sentence per column; slide 13 one output per bucket.

variants.name: emergency
variants.rules: Skip slides 4 and 11 as standalone sections; mention AI infrastructure in slide 5 and testing output in slide 12.

attention_drops.why: The regulatory/governance section is the heaviest cognitive load.
attention_drops.recovery: Move into Slide 8 with the concrete question `第一層和第二層差在哪裡？`

attention_drops.why: Testing can become a tool-list detour.
attention_drops.recovery: Use `但 finding 出來以後，真正的治理才開始。` and make Decision the visual center.

attention_drops.why: The speaker may rush if Slide 11 expands.
attention_drops.recovery: Cut testing examples, keep 30 / 60 / 90, and start Slide 14 by 27:40.

rubric_translation.design_goal: Make the talk feel inevitable from model to trust system.
rubric_translation.observable_success: Audience can restate the arc: product scale -> evidence -> testing -> Patch SLA -> trust.
rubric_translation.tactics: Use Slides 3, 5, 6, 12, and 14 as structural anchors.
rubric_translation.failure_patterns: Sections feel like independent lectures; closing introduces new content.
rubric_translation.avoid: Agenda-first opening, framework dumping, and late-stage new ideas.
rubric_translation.slide_influence: Every slide needs one governing message and a transition reason.
rubric_translation.speaking_influence: Name why the next section exists before moving.

rubric_translation.design_goal: Deliver technical substance without overwhelming the room.
rubric_translation.observable_success: Security, regulatory, product, and healthcare listeners each hear usable decisions.
rubric_translation.tactics: Translate FDA 524B, FDA 2025, TFDA-facing AI/ML SaMD evidence, testing, and Patch SLA into artifacts.
rubric_translation.failure_patterns: Tool-name dumping, regulation name-dropping, or shallow slogans.
rubric_translation.avoid: Long legal quotes, exploit steps, and vendor-style claims.
rubric_translation.slide_influence: Use evidence chains, stack diagrams, contrast panels, and decision flows.
rubric_translation.speaking_influence: Explain the artifact or decision each concept produces.

rubric_translation.design_goal: Vary pace and protect the ending.
rubric_translation.observable_success: Slide 7 ends near 14:00; Slide 14 begins by 27:40; content ends by 28:30.
rubric_translation.tactics: Slow on memory slides, accelerate through labels, and use Slide 10 as recovery.
rubric_translation.failure_patterns: Regulatory middle expands; speaker compensates by rushing the close.
rubric_translation.avoid: Trying to solve density by speaking faster.
rubric_translation.slide_influence: Dense slides must use progressive reveal; memory slides must breathe.
rubric_translation.speaking_influence: Use cut markers before time pressure becomes visible.

rubric_translation.observable_success: The speaker uses short sentences, consistent terms, and no apologetic hedging.
rubric_translation.tactics: Preserve the strong lines and deliver them with silence.
rubric_translation.failure_patterns: Reading slides, overexplaining acronyms, and adding examples after Slide 10.
rubric_translation.avoid: Passive classroom phrasing and filler transitions.
rubric_translation.speaking_influence: Use Taiwan Traditional Chinese with English terms only where they are domain-standard.

rubric_translation.design_goal: Make meaning visible in three seconds.
rubric_translation.observable_success: Audience sees a phrase, ladder, chain, stack, contrast, flow, or roadmap before explanation.
rubric_translation.tactics: Use one dominant visual object per slide and semantic color only.
rubric_translation.failure_patterns: Text blocks, repeated template layout, decorative hacker imagery, or generic AI symbolism.
rubric_translation.avoid: Stock visuals, random icons, and paragraph slides.
rubric_translation.slide_influence: Apple-style restraint, high contrast, and diagram-led meaning.
rubric_translation.speaking_influence: Refer to the visual only when it advances the decision.

rubric_translation.observable_success: Stillness and eye contact on Slides 3, 10, 12, and 14.
rubric_translation.tactics: Use posture changes only for structural transitions and gestures only for diagrams.
rubric_translation.failure_patterns: Looking back during strong lines, wandering, or visible time panic.
rubric_translation.avoid: Overmoving during patient-safety and closing moments.
rubric_translation.slide_influence: Minimize text that forces the speaker to read.
rubric_translation.speaking_influence: Pause after the strong lines and stop cleanly.

rubric_translation.design_goal: Make the talk memorable and useful after the conference.
rubric_translation.observable_success: Audience recalls `你賣的不是模型`, four product scales, Patch SLA, and `30 / 60 / 90`.
rubric_translation.tactics: Repeat memory anchors and convert them into action.
rubric_translation.failure_patterns: Interesting content with no operational next step.
rubric_translation.avoid: Broad best-practice endings and administrative closing clutter.
rubric_translation.speaking_influence: End on principle and ownership, not logistics.

scoring_categories.strengths: Strong myth-break opening, four-scale map, Patch SLA climax, and trust-before-audit close.
scoring_categories.weaknesses: Baseline version risks treating slides 6-7 as a middle lecture rather than one evidence bridge.
scoring_categories.evidence: Slides 3, 5, 6, 12, and 14 form a repeatable narrative spine.

scoring_categories.strengths: Specific evidence chain, AI stack boundaries, testing outputs, and Patch SLA decisions.
scoring_categories.weaknesses: Baseline testing section can sound like a tool survey if not tied to findings and retest evidence.
scoring_categories.evidence: FDA 524B verbs and FDA 2025 traceability are converted into artifacts and decisions.

scoring_categories.strengths: 28:30 content target with 1:30 buffer; Slide 10 recovery beat protects attention.
scoring_categories.weaknesses: Baseline can run late if slides 6-7 or 11 expand.
scoring_categories.evidence: Slide-level seconds total 1710 and define cut markers at slide 7, slide 12, and slide 14.

scoring_categories.strengths: Stage-language anchors are concise and credible.
scoring_categories.weaknesses: Baseline wording can become explanatory instead of decision-oriented in regulatory and testing sections.
scoring_categories.evidence: Strong lines: `你賣的不是模型`, `法規要的是證據，不是口號`, `Cyber Safety is Patient Safety`, `沒有 decision，就沒有治理；沒有證據，就沒有信任`.

scoring_categories.strengths: Deck uses statement, full visual, contrast, diagram, and progressive-build layouts.
scoring_categories.weaknesses: Baseline slides 6-7 require strict label control to avoid visual density.
scoring_categories.evidence: Memory slides 3, 5, 10, 12, and 14 are visually distinctive.

scoring_categories.strengths: Memory slides create obvious eye-contact and stillness moments.
scoring_categories.weaknesses: Time pressure can reduce composure if Slide 11 expands.
scoring_categories.evidence: Slides 3, 10, 12, and 14 are built for stillness and direct address.

scoring_categories.strengths: The talk gives memorable phrases plus a concrete 30 / 60 / 90 path.
scoring_categories.weaknesses: Baseline impact weakens if the final minute is rushed.
scoring_categories.evidence: Four-scale map, Patch SLA, and 30 / 60 / 90 roadmap provide post-session utility.

hard_rules: Exactly one controlling idea per slide, except the required disclaimer.
Headline or sentence text stays below 12 words; technical labels are allowed only when they function as diagram labels.
At least 70% of each slide is visual structure or whitespace.
No more than two bullets; prefer zero.
Audience should understand the point in under three seconds.
No private hospital/client detail, proprietary code, raw student records, exploit recipes, or patent-sensitive implementation mechanics.

design_system.style: Dark, clean, high-contrast, diagram-led, and restrained.
design_system.fonts: Modern sans: title and label styles only.
design_system.colors: One primary accent for evidence/decision and one risk color for interruption or unresolved exposure.
design_system.imagery: No hacker hoodies, AI brains, random circuit stock art, or dramatic hospital imagery.
design_system.density: One dominant visual object per slide; technical detail belongs in labels, speech, or Q&A.

penalties.condition: Small density, spacing, or consistency issue that does not block comprehension.
Hard-rule failure, AI-feel risk, or important slide losing visual hierarchy.
Text-heavy, no structure, unreadable, or public-safety boundary failure.

baseline_constraint_results.violations: Risk of legal-text density if FDA evidence is shown as source quotes instead of a chain.
baseline_constraint_results.redesign: Keep only Evidence, not slogans; Risk -> Control -> Test -> Fix -> Evidence; Monitor | CVD | Patch | SBOM.

baseline_constraint_results.violations: Three-column bridge can become a control catalog.
baseline_constraint_results.redesign: Reduce each column to one noun phrase plus three labels; speak the rest.

baseline_constraint_results.violations: Minor tool-catalog risk if examples expand.
baseline_constraint_results.redesign: Keep contrast between early repair and external exposure; output strip must say finding list + retest evidence.

baseline_constraint_results.violations: Minor risk that flow competes with Decision node.
baseline_constraint_results.redesign: Make Decision visually dominant and outcomes secondary.

slides.title: AI 軟體醫材的資安實戰
slides.message: This is a product-trust and medical-device cybersecurity talk, not an AI hype talk.
slides.audience_understanding: The talk connects FDA 524B, threat modeling, and Patch SLA into one implementation path.
slides.visual_concept: Restrained title slide with a faint product-system outline and one calm accent line.
onscreen: AI 軟體醫材的資安實戰
slides.transition_in: Walk on with calm posture; no biography expansion.
slides.transition_out: 關鍵字很多，但今天我只想留下三件事：信任、修補、證據。
slides.speaker_note: 各位好，我是林家聖。今天的題目是 AI 軟體醫材的資安實戰。

slides.title: Required CYBERSEC Disclaimer
slides.message: This compliance beat should be visible, brief, and separate from the narrative.
slides.audience_understanding: The speaker respects organizer rules and will move quickly into substance.
slides.transition_in: After title, hold the slide without commentary overload.
slides.transition_out: 接下來我們直接進入今天真正的問題。
slides.speaker_note: 這是大會必要的 disclaimer，我在這裡停一下，讓大家看見。接下來我們直接進入今天真正的問題。

slides.title: 你賣的不是模型
slides.message: AI SaMD teams sell a trustable, patchable, auditable system.
slides.audience_understanding: The talk is about accountability around the model, not model performance alone.
slides.visual_concept: Dominant phrase with three quiet anchors: Trust, Patch, Evidence.
slides.transition_in: Begin after the disclaimer with a direct frame break.
slides.transition_out: 如果這是真的，我們就要問：為什麼 2026 年這件事更急？
slides.speaker_note: 你賣的是一個在醫療情境中可被信任、可被修補、可被稽核的系統。

slides.title: AI 變成基礎設施
slides.message: When AI becomes infrastructure, cyber incidents can disrupt clinical care.
slides.audience_understanding: Medical AI security scope grows with deployment scope.
slides.visual_concept: Three-layer AI stack on the left; clinical continuity line with one interruption on the right.
slides.transition_in: Use the 2026 urgency question from Slide 3.
slides.transition_out: 所以我們需要一張產品尺度地圖，先決定風險到底長在哪裡。
slides.speaker_note: 醫療資安也已經不只是 IT 後台問題；當 AI 進入醫療，cyber incident 可能直接干擾 clinical care。

slides.title: Risk grows with architecture
slides.message: Product architecture determines attack surface and evidence burden.
slides.audience_understanding: The same AI model creates different cybersecurity obligations depending on product scale.
slides.visual_concept: Four-step ladder with an evidence-growth line.
slides.transition_in: Move from infrastructure risk to the scoping map.
slides.transition_out: 有了這張地圖，我們再看法規到底在要求什麼。
slides.speaker_note: 每加一層，產品能力變強，攻擊面也變大，證據需求也變大。

slides.title: Evidence, not slogans
slides.message: Regulation asks for traceable evidence, not claims.
slides.audience_understanding: Monitor/identify/address, CVD, updates/patches, SBOM, architecture, threat model, controls, tests, and finding disposition must connect.
slides.visual_concept: Horizontal evidence chain with four FDA 524B verbs as a side rail.
slides.transition_in: Use the product-scale map to ask what regulation actually requires.
slides.transition_out: 回到台灣和公司內部治理，這條證據鏈還需要兩種語言：模型語言和管理語言。
slides.speaker_note: 法規要的是證據，不是口號。這條線接不起來，文件再多也只是資料夾。

slides.title: Model, governance, stack
slides.message: A credible team can explain the model, govern risk, and secure the stack.
slides.audience_understanding: Model evidence alone is not enough; governance and stack boundaries must be clear.
slides.visual_concept: Three-column bridge: model evidence, governance language, AI stack security.
slides.transition_in: Translate the evidence chain into Taiwan-team communication layers.
slides.transition_out: 接下來回到剛才的四層產品尺度，看每一層到底多了什麼風險。
slides.speaker_note: NIST 在這裡是共同語言，不是控制清單；AI RMF 講 Govern, Map, Measure, Manage，CSF 可補上資安營運語言。

slides.title: Scale 1-2
slides.message: A wrapper turns a model artifact into an attackable user-facing system.
slides.audience_understanding: Scale 1 needs artifact/data/dependency/update evidence; Scale 2 adds parser, upload, cache, and output risk.
slides.visual_concept: Two-panel comparison with the same label grammar.
slides.transition_in: Recover attention with a concrete scale question.
slides.transition_out: 如果再往上長成平台，資安就不只是產品問題，而是公司營運和臨床連續性問題。
slides.speaker_note: Model 到 viewer 的差別，是產品從一個 artifact 變成一個使用情境。

slides.title: Scale 3-4
slides.message: Platform and hospital integration make cybersecurity a business and patient-safety issue.
slides.audience_understanding: Scale 3 introduces identity/API/database/audit log/backup; Scale 4 introduces hospital network and service continuity.
slides.visual_concept: Two-panel system map: product platform to connected clinical environment.
slides.transition_in: Escalate from user-facing risk to operating and continuity risk.
slides.transition_out: 所以醫療 AI 的資安，最後不是一句 IT 口號，而是一句病人安全的話。
slides.speaker_note: 這時候 cyber risk 不只是產品保護，而是 clinical continuity risk。

slides.title: Cyber Safety Is Patient Safety
slides.message: Medical-device cybersecurity is part of patient safety.
slides.audience_understanding: Security failures can affect patients, hospital operations, supplier responsibility, and clinical workflow.
slides.visual_concept: Full-screen phrase with strong negative space and no framework text.
slides.transition_in: Step into the ethical peak after the scale 4 continuity point.
slides.transition_out: 接下來進入最實作的部分：怎麼測，怎麼修，怎麼留下證據。
slides.speaker_note: Cyber Safety is Patient Safety. Pause.

slides.title: White-box + Black-box
slides.message: White-box finds repairable problems early; black-box validates external exposure.
slides.audience_understanding: Both testing modes must produce findings and retest evidence.
slides.visual_concept: Inside-out versus outside-in contrast with one output strip.
slides.transition_in: Move from patient safety to how risks become actionable findings.
slides.transition_out: 但 finding 出來以後，真正的治理才開始。
slides.speaker_note: Testing 的價值，是讓風險變成可以修、可以驗證、可以追蹤的 finding。

slides.title: Patch SLA
slides.message: Every security finding needs ownership, a decision, repair path, retest, and archived evidence.
slides.audience_understanding: Patch SLA is governance, not ticket hygiene.
slides.visual_concept: Finding journey with Decision as the dominant node.
slides.transition_in: Use the testing output strip to ask what happens after findings appear.
slides.transition_out: 我知道很多小團隊會覺得這很大，所以最後把它壓成 30、60、90 天。
slides.speaker_note: 沒有 decision，就沒有治理；沒有證據，就沒有信任。

slides.title: Small Team 30 / 60 / 90
slides.message: Start with minimum evidence, then repeatable workflow, then external validation.
slides.audience_understanding: A small team can start without pretending to have a large enterprise program.
slides.visual_concept: Three-step timeline with equal visual weight.
onscreen: Threat model / CI gates / finding workflow
Pen test / Patch SLA / retest evidence
must_not_show: More than three deliverables per bucket
slides.transition_in: Lower the barrier after the Patch SLA climax.
slides.transition_out: 最後，我們回到今天的第一個問題：怎麼在稽核之前先建立信任。
slides.speaker_note: 重點不是三個月變完美，而是三個月後，公司開始自動留下可信證據。

slides.title: 先建立信任，再面對稽核
slides.message: Build security into the product so regulatory documents become evidence, not theater.
slides.audience_understanding: Mature teams prove, repair, and track risk before audit pressure.
slides.visual_concept: Large closing phrase with quiet space and no new content.
onscreen: 法規文件才會自然長出來
slides.transition_in: Return to the opening thesis and close the loop.
slides.speaker_note: 真正成熟的團隊，不是文件最多的團隊，而是每一個風險都知道怎麼證明、怎麼修、怎麼追的團隊。謝謝大家。

top_failure_points: Slides 6-7 can become dense if they carry regulatory detail that belongs in speech.
Slide 11 can drift into a testing-tool catalog instead of finding governance.
If Slide 7 ends after 14:30, the close becomes vulnerable.
Visual repetition can occur if comparison slides and process slides share the same generic grid.
Audience impact drops if the final principle is delivered after time pressure appears.
top_improvements: Rewrite Slide 6 as an evidence chain, not a legal text block.
Reduce Slide 7 to three columns with three labels each and move explanation to speech.
Make Slide 12's Decision node the largest object in the flow.
Use Slide 10 as silence, not content expansion.
Rehearse the safe version until Slide 14 reliably starts by 27:40.
improved_outline: Thesis: not model; trust, patch, evidence.
Map: four product scales determine evidence burden.
Evidence: FDA 524B and FDA 2025 become traceability.
Application: risk grows from model to connected medical system.
Peak: cyber safety is patient safety.
Operation: testing creates findings; Patch SLA creates decisions.
optimization.improved_opening: 各位好，我是林家聖。今天有很多關鍵字：FDA 524B、Threat Modeling、Patch SLA。但我想先用一句話把它們接起來：你賣的不是模型。
optimization.improved_closing: 真正成熟的團隊，不是文件最多的團隊，而是每一個風險都知道怎麼證明、怎麼修、怎麼追的團隊。先把安全做進產品，法規文件才會自然長出來。
improved_transitions: Slide 5 to 6: 有了產品尺度，法規就不是抽象要求，而是這一層產品要留下哪些證據。
Slide 7 to 8: 現在把治理語言放回產品尺度，看看每一層多了什麼攻擊面。
Slide 10 to 11: 如果 cyber safety 是 patient safety，testing 就不是工具展示，而是把風險變成可修補的 finding。
Slide 11 to 12: 但 finding 出來以後，真正的治理才開始。
Slide 13 to 14: Roadmap 的目的不是做更多文件，而是在稽核之前先建立信任。

memorable_moments.purpose: Turn technical risk into healthcare consequence.

memorable_moments.line: 沒有 decision，就沒有治理；沒有證據，就沒有信任。
memorable_moments.purpose: Make Patch SLA the operational climax.
